meta.subtitle: Plain-English market read · Gap-risk check · Chip selloff goes global · PCE Thursday
meta.rerun_note: Core PCE inflation report Thu 6/25 8:30 AM · re-run after

verdict.label: YELLOW · Trade small or sit out
verdict.desc: A global chip selloff is hitting the tape this morning: South Korea's KOSPI crashed about 10% — its worst day in months — as investors dumped semiconductors on fears the AI rally got overheated. U.S. futures are lower, led by tech. Oil keeps sliding toward $73 on Iran peace progress, and volatility is creeping up (VIX ~17.3). On top of that, FedEx reports tonight and Thursday's Core PCE inflation report is the week's big test. Take one small, well-supported trade at most, or wait for the inflation read.

composite.band_note: MILD · up from 41 · global chip selloff + PCE ahead

gap.band_note: Below the danger line · but rising on the chip rout; bigger risk is Thursday's PCE

focus.jbig: Global chip selloff · digesting a hawkish Fed
focus.jtag: Korea's KOSPI crashes ~10% on chips; oil falls; PCE lands Thursday
focus.jnote: Today is a jittery "wait for the data" day. The big new story is overseas: South Korea's KOSPI plunged about 10% — its steepest drop in over three months — as foreign investors dumped chip stocks on signals the sector's rally had gotten overheated; Samsung and SK Hynix each fell more than 12%, triggering a trading halt. That selloff is bleeding into U.S. tech futures. Oil keeps sliding toward $73 on Iran peace progress, but last week's hawkish Fed still hangs over everything — traders now price a possible 2026 rate hike and the 10-year yield sits near 4.5%. FedEx reports after the close tonight and Core PCE inflation lands Thursday, so size down.
focus.notdown: Industrials and financials (banks BofA, JPM up ~2% yesterday) held the Dow firm, but that's rotation, not a single named catalyst, and a global risk-off wave could reverse it. Low grade.
focus.notup: Energy (XLE, XOM, CVX, OXY) stays the cleaner bearish read: oil is sliding toward $73 on the Iran 60-day roadmap and Gulf supply hopes. Real, durable news. Semis (SMH, NVDA, AVGO, MU) are now dropping hard on the global chip rout — but shorting a fast-falling group on momentum (not a named catalyst) into MU earnings is risky. Energy half size; semis watch only.
focus.why: Why YELLOW and not GREEN? The gap-risk numbers (46 and 27%) are still calm enough for a green light on paper — but three rules apply: respect a fresh policy shock (the Fed just flipped from cuts to possible hikes), don't short a fast-falling market on pure momentum, and don't go full size right before a major inflation print. Core PCE lands Thursday 8:30 AM and FedEx reports tonight. Take one small, well-supported trade if it checks out at 9:35 AM, or wait. A cleaner read returns after PCE.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~17.3, calm but ticking up on the chip rout
Big events (next 1–3 days) — Core PCE Thursday + flash PMIs today + FedEx earnings tonight
World risk — global chip selloff (KOSPI −10%); Iran roadmap calming, but no signed deal yet
Volatility trend — ticking back up after easing post-Fed (estimated)
Commodities — oil down ~1%, which is calming for inflation, not scary

setups.read: Best idea. Oil is sliding toward $73 on the Iran 60-day roadmap, Hormuz/supply hopes, and Gulf producers ramping output — that caps the whole group. Real, durable news, but it's pre-PCE, so trade half size.

Falling hard on a global chip rout (KOSPI −10%, Samsung/SK Hynix −12%) after a historic run, but that's momentum, not a clean U.S. catalyst. Micron (MU) reports Wednesday — earnings risk inside the window. Don't short into it. Wait.

Reports AMC tonight — earnings risk is in front of it, not behind it. A spread now would straddle the print (~7% implied move). Can't use it until the gap is set; revisit tomorrow as a possible Type A.
Yesterday's leaders with banks up ~2%, but it's broad rotation with no single news story, and a global risk-off wave plus a hawkish Fed could reverse it fast. Wait.
Newly public, ultra-volatile (down a 3rd straight day toward ~$147 on a $20B+ bond sale): no real options history, no stable support, wild swings. Can't use it.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Choppy, tech-heavy digestion ahead of PCE
scenarios.sub: Chip selloff weighs on the Nasdaq; Dow steadier; everyone waits for Thursday's inflation read

scenarios.label: Bad case — Chip rout deepens, tech rolls over
scenarios.sub: The global semi selloff feeds U.S. tech and hike fears stick → broad risk-off, Nasdaq leads down

scenarios.label: Good case — Dip-buyers step in, oil keeps falling
scenarios.sub: Chip washout is treated as healthy; cheaper oil eases inflation worry → relief bounce

scenarios.label: Surprise — Iran stumbles or a shock hits
scenarios.sub: The 60-day roadmap breaks down, FedEx shocks, or a hot data point lands → oil and volatility jump

risks: Core PCE inflation · Thu 6/25 8:30 AM — the Fed's favorite inflation gauge; with inflation at a three-year high, a hot print could push yields and 2026 hike odds higher.
Global chip selloff — KOSPI crashed ~10% with Samsung and SK Hynix down 12%+; that overheated-AI fear is bleeding into U.S. semis (NVDA, AVGO, MU). Don't short the group on momentum alone.
FedEx (FDX) earnings tonight — first report since the Freight spin-off, with a ~7% implied move; don't sell spreads on FDX into the print.
Micron (MU) earnings Wednesday — adds gap risk to the whole memory/semi complex inside a spread's window.
Hike fears · short end firm — markets price a possible 2026 Fed hike with the 10-year near 4.5%; growth and tech can drop fast if yields keep climbing.
Iran deal not signed yet — it's a 60-day roadmap, and Iran says the Strait of Hormuz "will never return" to pre-war terms; oil could whipsaw on any setback.

rows.today: Risk-off: tech-led drop, chip rout global
